questions.question: 1. If the receiving end voltage is greater than the sending end voltage, regulation of transmission line is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Positive regulation means Vr < Vs.
explanations.b: Correct. Regulation = (Vs - Vr)/Vr × 100%. If Vr > Vs, regulation is negative (Ferranti effect).

questions.question: 2. The charging current in the transmission line:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Charging current leads voltage in a capacitor.
explanations.c: Correct. Charging current flows through the shunt capacitance and leads the voltage by 90°.

questions.question: 3. The reflection coefficient of an open-ended transmission line is:

explanations.a: Correct. For open-ended line, reflection coefficient Γ = (Z_load - Zc)/(Z_load + Zc) = (∞ - Zc)/(∞ + Zc) = 1.

questions.question: 4. Ferranti effect is experienced on a long overhead transmission lines when it is:

explanations.a: Correct. Ferranti effect (receiving end voltage > sending end voltage) occurs under light load or no-load conditions due to charging current.

questions.question: 5. The power loss in a transmission line due to charging current is:

explanations.a: Incorrect. Full Ic²R would be if all charging current flowed through full resistance.
explanations.c: Correct. Since charging current is distributed along the line, effective loss is approximately Ic²R/2.
